---
title: "Monotony and stability analysis"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior 

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format: 
  html: 
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em

jupyter: python3

---



```{python}

import pandas as pd

import importlib
import src.data_analysis.monotony_stability as utils

importlib.reload(utils)
from src.data_analysis.monotony_stability import*

from config import data_path, src_path, data_output_path

```


```{python}
# Impoort data from datasets
path_datasets = data_path
data_cleaning_output_path = data_output_path
```


```{python}
# Import data from datasets
train_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/train_imputed.csv")
train_imputed.head()
test_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/test_imputed.csv")

oot_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/oot_imputed.csv")
```



```{python}



   
fig = plot_default_by_bin(
    df=train_imputed,
    default_var="def",
    continuous_var="loan_percent_income",
    year_var="year",
    bins=3,   # ← terciles
    title="Montoney study of Loan Percent income",
)
#fig.savefig("/mnt/user-data/outputs/default_terciles.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

```

 
 ```{python}
 # ── Application
    
fig = plot_default_by_category(
    df=train_imputed,
    default_var="def",
    qualitative_var="cb_person_default_on_file",
    year_var="year",
    title="Monotony Historical default",
)
#fig.savefig("/mnt/user-data/outputs/default_categories.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
 ```

 # Create the home_ownership_3 variable


 ```{python}

mapping = {
    'OWN': 'OWN',
    'MORTGAGE' : 'MORTGAGE',
    "RENT": "OTHER_RENT", 
    "OTHER": "OTHER_RENT"
}
train_imputed["home_ownership_3"] = train_imputed["person_home_ownership"].map(mapping)

test_imputed["home_ownership_3"] = test_imputed["person_home_ownership"].map(mapping)

oot_imputed["home_ownership_3"] = oot_imputed["person_home_ownership"].map(mapping)

fig = plot_default_by_category(
        df=train_imputed,
        default_var="def",
        qualitative_var="home_ownership_3",
        year_var="year",
        title="Monotony Home ownership 3",
    )
    #fig.savefig("/mnt/user-data/outputs/default_categories.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

 ```





 ```{python}


# ── Application

result = compute_psi_table(
    df=train_imputed,
    continuous_vars=["person_income", "person_emp_length", "loan_int_rate",
    "loan_percent_income"],
    qualitative_vars=["home_ownership_3", "cb_person_default_on_file"],
    year_var="year",
    bins=3,
)

print(result.to_string())
result.to_excel(f"{path_datasets}/year_to_year.xlsx")
 ```



 ```{python}



# ── Application

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # ── Cas nominal ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    result = compute_psi_stability(
        train=train_imputed, test=test_imputed, oot=oot_imputed,
        continuous_vars= ["person_income", "person_emp_length", "loan_int_rate",
        "loan_percent_income"],
        qualitative_vars=["home_ownership_3", "cb_person_default_on_file"],
        bins=3,
    )
    print("── Résultat nominal ──")
    print(result.to_string(), "\n")

    # ── Démonstration des erreurs ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    print("── Test erreur (bin vide dans REF ou CURR) ──")
    try:
        tiny_train = _make_df(20, seed=99)   # trop petit → bins vides probables
        compute_psi_stability(
            train=tiny_train, test=test, oot=oot,
            continuous_vars=["income"], qualitative_vars=[], bins=3,
        )
    except (ZeroDivisionError, ValueError) as e:
        print(e, "\n")

    result.to_excel(f"{path_datasets}/psi_stability.xlsx")
 ```



 ```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def plot_default_rate(df, variable, target='default', bins=5):
    """
    Plot le taux de défaut par bin d'une variable continue.
    
    Paramètres:
    -----------
    df       : DataFrame pandas
    variable : str, nom de la variable continue
    target   : str, nom de la variable cible (défaut = 'default')
    bins     : int, nombre de bins égaux (défaut = 5)
    """
    
    # Découpage en bins égaux
    df_copy = df.copy()
    df_copy['bin'] = pd.cut(df_copy[variable], bins=bins)
    
    # Calcul du taux de défaut par bin
    taux = df_copy.groupby('bin', observed=True)[target].mean().reset_index()
    taux.columns = ['bin', 'default_rate']
    
    # Label lisible pour l'axe X
    taux['bin_label'] = taux['bin'].astype(str)
    
    # Plot
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
    
    ax.plot(taux['bin_label'], taux['default_rate'],
            color='gray', linewidth=1.5,
            marker='D', markersize=6,
            markerfacecolor='gray', markeredgecolor='gray')
    
    ax.set_title(f'Default Rate versus {variable}', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel(variable)
    ax.set_ylabel('Default Rate')
    
    ax.yaxis.grid(True, color='lightgray', linewidth=0.8)
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)
    ax.xaxis.grid(False)
    
    ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
    
    plt.xticks(rotation=30, ha='right')
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    
    return taux


# --- Exemple d'utilisation ---
np.random.seed(42)
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'age':     np.random.randint(15, 80, size=10000),
    'income':  np.random.normal(50000, 15000, size=10000),
    'default': np.random.binomial(1, 0.02, size=10000)
})

plot_default_rate(train_imputed, variable='person_income', target='def', bins=10)
plot_default_rate(train_imputed, variable='person_emp_length', target='def', bins=10)
plot_default_rate(train_imputed, variable='loan_int_rate', target='def', bins=10)
plot_default_rate(train_imputed, variable='loan_percent_income', target='def', bins=10)

 ```
